title: Async Context, Abort Signals, Deadlines, and Cleanup · Inference Brief: Abortsignal Timeout Any
summary: Request context is stored globally, promises continue after disconnect, timeouts race without cancellation, dependency budgets consume cleanup time, and rejected work becomes unhandled. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts abortsignal timeout any increment in request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose client disconnect, service deadline, dependency timeout, and shutdown with AbortSignal.timeout and AbortSignal.any while preserving the first cause.
Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6593 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; show recovery after the overload input is removed; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
